steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: After "investigate how," the blank must complete an indirect question — a subordinate noun clause that acts as the object of the verb. Indirect questions use statement word order (subject before auxiliary verb) and end with a period, not a question mark.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — inverted word order with question mark: flips auxiliary before subject, treating this as a direct question. Indirect questions never use direct-question word order. ✗
  • Choice B — statement order with period: keeps subject before auxiliary ("their use could be better regulated") and ends with a period. Correct indirect question structure. ✓
  • Choice C — inverted word order with period: drops the question mark but keeps the inverted auxiliary-first order, which is still wrong for an indirect question. ✗
  • Choice D — statement order with question mark: gets the word order right but ends with a question mark, which turns the whole sentence into a direct question. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
